title: Pentanema parietarioides
taxonomy: Pentanema parietarioides is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Pentanema, which is a group of plants that were previously classified as part of the genus Anthemis. The plant is native to parts of Europe and has been identified as a species with distinctive characteristics. Pentanema parietarioides is further classified into the tribe Inuleae, which is a group of plants that share similar traits. Further classification involves its order, class, and kingdom, placing it within the plant kingdom, specifically in the family Asteraceae.

intro: Pentanema parietarioides is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae), native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Pentanema parietarioides include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
